And they still need a strategy to get the whole process along.
And so those three components, like which is like the strategy, the tech, and the humans in the loop and expertise, think of it as like the execution.
Like you need all three components.

Right.

And that's what, you know, Daniel and the team and what we built under the hood is kind of this, call it like, I like to think of it as like an AI workflow as code, you know.

But then when I started to go through the workflows and what we build and how we're learning from every run and how there's experts in the loop that guide that process along and how like there's no way to gain the same productivity that developers have gained in knowledge work unless it's represented in a way that you can actually run and learn from.
And so like, just to back it up a little bit, right?
Like, if you think about like a software developer engineer like Daniel, how much more productive is Daniel today than he was 10 years ago or even 15 years ago pre-Github?

And there's things to test it and run it, and there's CI and CD, and there's, like, infrastructure's code, and there's, like, all kinds of things.

into something that non-developers can use.
